observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and
b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.
We recommended and the management agreed to implement the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended and the management agreed to submit the copies of contract for the implementation of all its structural projects within five (5) working days from the execution for contract review and inspection in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the municipal accountant to:
a. Request the depository bank (LBP – Rosary Heights Branch,
Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the BSs within
five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic
copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate
preparation of monthly BRS; and
b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to
ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank
account, pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996.

Therefore, we recommended that the LCE direct:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct yearly physical count of all PPEs
owned by the Municipality, pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No.
92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 114 of MNGAS, for
LGUs, Volume I.
b. The Accounting Division and the General Services Division or the
Designated Property and Supply Officer to maintain the PPELC and
PC, respectively, and a periodic reconciliation be made to detect any
discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records and
reflected in the book of accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to
record the transfer of the cost of completed projects booked as PPE under the Trust Fund to the General Fund.

3.5 The municipal accountant committed to make the necessary adjustments in
the records of all PPEs.

In view of the foregoing lapses, we recommended and Management agreed to
submit the copies of contract for the implementation of all its structural projects
within five (5) working days from the execution for contract review and inspection
in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009.

We recommended and the management agreed to implement the perpetual
inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with Section 114 of the
New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommend that the management faithfully adhere to the
limitations prescribed under Section 325(h) of the RA 7160 for
the allocation and utilization of funds for EMEs.

We recommended the management to faithfully adhere to Section 43 of
RA 9710 which requires that GAD budget shall be at least five percent
(5%) of their total budgetary allocation.

We recommended the LCE to direct the municipal accountant to
record all related transactions completely by transferring the amount
of ₱4,182,179.45 to the Trust Liability-DRMM account in the Trust
Fund, pursuant to Paragraph 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the management agreed to procure common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM in compliance to Section 53.5 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of the Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act and Section 4 of the Administrative Order (AO) No. 17 dated July 28, 201. If the same were not available, secure certification of non-availability of the items being procured.
We recommended and the management agreed to direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances.

We recommend the following: a. Management should conduct a regular physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment, pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997; b. A periodic reconciliation of the PPE ledger cards maintained by the accountant with that of the Property Cards maintained by the Supply Officer should be regularly made to promptly detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be out rightly recorded and reflected in the book of accounts;
observations.managementAction: No action taken

observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines –
Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with
the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the
month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions
of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and
b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to
ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank
account.

2.5. Therefore, we reiterated the following recommendations to the
management:
a. Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs
owned by the Municipality, pursuant to Section 4.3 of COA Circular
No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997, Section 156 of Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 114 of
the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for
Local Government Units, Volume I.
b. PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the
Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a
periodic reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any
discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be
recorded and reflected in the book of accounts.

3.9 We recommended and the management agreed to implement the
perpetual inventory method in recording purchases in accordance with Section 114 of the
New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

4.5 We recommended and the management agreed to procure common-use
supplies directly from the PS-DBM in compliance to Section 53.5 of the
2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of the
Republic Act No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government
Procurement Reform Act and Section 4 of the Administrative Order
(AO) No. 17 dated July 28, 201. If the same were not available, secure
certification of non-availability of the items being procured.

5.5 We recommended and the management agreed to submit the copies of
contract for the implementation of all its structural projects within five
(5) working days from the execution for contract review and inspection
in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

6.2 We recommended and the management agreed to direct all responsible
persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into
disallowances.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to: a. Abide to Section 74 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 3.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; b. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and c. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: Regularly requested the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and BRS was submitted.

observations.managementAction: The necessary disclosures of the PPE were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: All Notice of Suspension issued were settled by the responsible person.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: a. Management should conduct a regular physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment, pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997; b. A periodic reconciliation of the PPE ledger cards maintained by the accountant with that of the Property Cards maintained by the Supply Officer should be regularly made to promptly detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts; c. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the above-mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements, in accordance with paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation

1.7. We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant
to:

a. Abide to Section 74 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known
as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 3.2 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996;
b. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights
Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank
statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for
electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate
preparation of monthly BRS; and
c. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the
correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

Recommendation

2.5. We recommended to the management the following:

a. Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned
by the Municipality, pursuant to Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005
dated July 10, 1997, Section 156 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular
No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 114 of the Manual on the
New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Units, Volume I.
b. PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the
Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic
reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and
appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected
in the book of accounts.

Recommendation

3.3 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should include the above-
mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements, in accordance with
paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 -
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

Recommendation

4.3. We recommended that the management should direct all responsible persons to
settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to
Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to: a. Abide to Section 74 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Section 3.2 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; b. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and c. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

We recommended that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to fully disclose the LDRRMF, in accordance with Section 5.1.16 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the management should implement the 20% Development Fund by focusing on the three (3) development projects in the area of social, economic and environmental management, in accordance with Section 3.3 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommend the following: a. Management should conduct a regular physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment, pursuant to the provision of Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997; b. A periodic reconciliation of the PPE ledger cards maintained by the accountant with that of the Property Cards maintained by the Supply Officer should be regularly made to promptly detect any discrepancies and appropriate adjustments in both records should be out rightly recorded and reflected in the book of accounts; c. Management should comply strictly with the aforementioned provisions of PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: No action taken

to:

b. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights
Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank
statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for
electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate
preparation of monthly BRS; and
c. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the
correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

2.5. We recommended to the management the following:
a. Inventory Committee should conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned
by the Municipality, pursuant to Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005
dated July 10, 1997, Section 156 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular
No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 114 of the Manual on the
New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Units, Volume I.
b. PPE ledger cards and Property Cards should be maintained by the
Accounting Division and by the General Services Division, and a periodic
reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and
appropriate adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected
in the book of accounts.

Recommendation:
3.3 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should include the above-
mentioned disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements, in accordance with
paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 17 -
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

Recommendation:
4.3. We recommended that the management should direct all responsible persons to
settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances, pursuant to
Section 9.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009.

Recommendation:
5.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to fully disclose the
LDRRMF, in accordance with Section 5.1.16 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular

11.4 We recommended that the management should implement the 20% Development
Fund by focusing on the three (3) development projects in the area of social, economic
and environmental management, in accordance with Sections 3 and 3.3 of Department

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to: a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

The Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Notes to Financial Statements and Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as required by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) as an additional statement to the regular Financial Statements to be submitted at year end. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to us the complete Notes to Financial Statements, pursuant to Section 109 of the Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

1.7. We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights
Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements
within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies
(snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly
BRS; and
b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the
correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

2.8 We reiterated the following recommendations to the Municipal Mayor:
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE by type
and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of
each year.
b) Require the Accountant and the GSO to prepare/maintain PPE ledger cards and
property cards, respectively, investigate any discrepancy noted, reconcile balances with the
Inventory Report, trace and account properties acquired in the vouchers and SL/GL to obtain
necessary information or data needed and set-up the required depreciation; and
c) Create an Appraisal Committee to evaluate and determine the current value of
prior years’ PPE acquisitions without available records.

3.5 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to
record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose
the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly BRS; and
b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the correct and accurate year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

observations.managementAction: a CPA was hired and prepared the reports needed

We reiterated our recommendations that the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Request the depository bank (Land Bank of the Philippines – Rosary Heights
Branch, Cotabato City) to provide the agency with the copy of the bank statements
within five days after the end of the month, and request for electronic copies
(snapshots) of transactions of bank accounts to facilitate preparation of monthly

b. Prepare and submit monthly BRS within the prescribed period to ensure the

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Inventory Committee to
conduct yearly inventory of all PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to the provision of
Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-005 dated July 10, 1997, and PPE ledger cards should be
maintained by the Accounting Division and General Services Division and a periodic
reconciliation should be regularly made to detect any discrepancies and appropriate
adjustments in both records should be recorded and reflected in the book of accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to
record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose
the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to (a) fully implement its Solid Waste
Management Plan with a clear guideline on the proper segregation and disposal of wastes; and
(b) extend assistance to the barangays for the construction of their own MRF.

We recommend that the Municipality of Sultan Mastura Management should strictly
observe the deadline for the remittance of the deductions to the GSIS as set in Section 14.1 of
the IRR of RA No. 8291, in order to avoid the penalties of 2% and to enable its officials and
employees in availing of the benefits and privileges offered by the GSIS to state workers.

We recommend that the Management should strictly observe the deadline of remittance
set forth in Rule VII, Section 3 of IRR of RA No. 9679 in order to avoid unnecessary incurrence
of penalties. The payment of penalties is a loss to the government which should have been used
in other priority programs for the betterment of the constituents of the municipality.

We recommend that the Management should promptly remit the deductions to the
Philhealth to enable its officials and employees avail the benefits and privileges offered by the
Philhealh to state workers.

We recommend that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the
Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances.

We recommend that the Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as required by the
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) and hire a qualified individual to fill
the vacant position of Municipal Accountant pursuant to Civil Service Commission’s minimum
qualification standard and that is a Certified Public Accountant (CPA).

standards that -

b) For the Accountant to regularly prepare
and submit the monthly Bank

submit the same to the Office of the
Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the
end of each month as prescribed under
COA Circular No. 92-125 A dated March
4, 1992.

We recommend that the management should
direct all responsible persons to settle the
Notice of Suspensions issued before
maturing into disallowances.

We recommend that the Chief Executive
should direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the Notes to Financial Statements
and Statement of Comparison of Budget and
Actual Amounts as required by the
Philippine Public Sector Accounting
Standards (PPSAS).

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
21.	We recommend the management to comply with rules and regulations on cash management and accounting standards that -
22.	a)  The Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements 	(BRS) as at   Year End and on a monthly basis, as provided under Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.	.
23.	b)For the Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-125 A dated March 4, 1992.

Recommendation:
44. 	We recommend that the management should direct all responsible persons to settle the Notice of Suspensions issued before maturing into disallowances.

Recommendation:
50.	We commend theMunicipality of Sultan Mastura Management for the effort of paying all of their 	GSIS 	remittances totaling to P 3,801,572.27 during the year, in compliance with Section 14 of the revised IRR, RA 8291; Section 5 paragraph (c) of RA 8291 and Article 183, Chapter IV, Section 1 of PD 626 as amended, thus enabling its officials and employees in readily availing of the benefits and privileges offered by the Government Service Insurance System.  We also recommended that Management continue the practice of remitting their GSIS obligations on time, to avoid payment of penalties/surcharges either due to non-remittance or delayed remittance, pursuant to Section 3, Rule V of the amended Rules on Employees’ Compensation.

Recommendation:
59.	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to record all related transactions completely, properly classify related accounts, and fully disclose the LDRRMF in accordance with the stated accounting guidelines.

Recommendation:
62.	We commend the management for implementing the 20% Economic Development Component of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) utilization on the program/projects to boost economic activities within the areas of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
72. 	We commend the management for setting aside ₱ 9,055,300.00 or 5% of their FY 2016 budget for Gender and Development (GAD), under Republic Act No. 10651 or FY 2016 General Appropriation Act, and for satisfactorily achieving its targets during the year.

Recommendation:
77.	We recommend that the Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Notes to Financial Statements and Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as required by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSA).

Recommendation:
83.	We commend the LGU – Sultan Mastura, Maguindanao for their formulation of the different plans required of them as bases for the development of their area in compliance with RA 7160 and DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1.
We recommend that the local government officials concerned should comply and adhere strictly with existing laws, rules and regulations in the disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to settle the balance per reconciliation of the records with the Auditor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: The management of the audited agency should submit report of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (LDRRM) Plan and the utilization of LDRRMF to the Office of the Auditor. Failure of the management to submit the same shall be a ground of any appropriate action against the responsible official(s).
observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit the LDRRM Plan for CYs 2013-2016.

observations.recommendation: The accountant or the management of the audited agency must cause the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same with all the supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The accountant submitted the bank reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: The management should prepare GAD Plan and Budget and submit the same to the COA Audit Team. Continued failures of the management of the Audited Agency to comply with the above cited Circulars shall be a ground to an appropriate action against the officials concerned
observations.managementAction: The management was able to submit the GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the personnel concerned should settle the Notice of Suspension within 90 days before maturing into disallowance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Notice of Suspensions issued were settled by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Property, Plant and Equipment pursuant to Section 59 of PPSAS-17 (Property, Plant and Equipment).
	
The adjusting journal entry:
		
Depreciation xx
 Accum. Dep.    xx

To take up Depreciation and set up Accumulated Depreciation
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension, pursuant to COA Circular No.2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to settle all the Notice of Suspensions issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to fully implement the 20% Economic Development Component of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) utilization on the program/projects to boost economic activities within the areas of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management fully implemented the 20% Component of IRA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to submit the report on the remaining GAD issue pertaining on the 13 barangay beneficiaries of the Philhealth Premium-MEDICARE Para sa Masa.
observations.managementAction: The GAD was fully implemented
